items.title: Bezos' Blue Origin valued at $130 billion in first public fundraising round
items.description: Bezos' Blue Origin is raising outside capital in a round valuing the rocket company at $130 billion, sources told CNBC's Andrew Ross Sorkin.

items.title: Trump says he doesn't want anything to do with Spain: 'Cut off all trade'
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump slammed Spain for not contributing enough to NATO, as he attended the defense alliance summit in Turkey.

items.title: Fed meeting minutes to show 'family fight' over rates. The squabble could drag on for a while
items.description: There have been few instances over the past 35 years or so when the Fed has only made one rate move, be it up or down.

items.title: Healthy Returns: Employers aren't expanding coverage of GLP-1 obesity drugs – many are finding ways around it
items.description: Around 36% of employers said they provide coverage of GLP-1s for both diabetes and weight loss, the same as 2025 and up from 34% in 2024, the survey said.

items.title: Delta launches 'basic business' fares without lounge access, seat selection
items.description: Delta is launching basic business and other stripped-down premium classes that come with fewer perks.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal until 2027 after latest setback, Kalshi traders predict
items.description: Speculators now see just a 44% chance that traffic flows return to normal by Dec. 1.

items.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Wednesday
items.description: Oil is surging, and Apple announces more investments into U.S. manufacturing.

items.title: OpenAI to publicly release GPT-5.6 AI models, ending government-requested limits
items.description: OpenAI's chief rival, Anthropic, recently restored access to its latest models following a weeks-long clash with the government.

items.title: Weekly mortgage demand drops as rates remain stuck in a narrow range
items.description: Mortgage rates barely moved last week and haven't moved much in more than a month. That is resulting in lackluster mortgage demand.

items.title: Trump pours cold water on NATO allies' united front
items.description: Trump criticized NATO allies over defense spending, attacked Spain and renewed his Greenland push during the alliance’s summit in Turkey.

items.title: Apple commits $30 billion to Broadcom for U.S. chipmaking push
items.description: Apple is expanding its Broadcom partnership in a $30 billion-plus chipmaking agreement, its largest American manufacturing commitment to date.

items.title: Trump opposes Carroll getting $5 million damages verdict, cites last-ditch Supreme Court bid
items.description: Trump has been found liable in two trials for defaming E. Jean Carroll when he denied her claim he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

items.title: Trump doubles down on push for control over Greenland as Denmark vows to defend it
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the U.S. needs to control Greenland “for the protection of the world."

items.title: OpenAI secures U.S. regulatory green light for GPT-5.6 rollout, Axios report says
SambaNova hits $11 billion valuation as investors back Nvidia chip challengers
items.description: SambaNova is valued at $11 billion after fresh financing led by General Atlantic, as AI chip startups look to challenge Nvidia.

items.title: Singapore's Temasek hits record portfolio value, eyes more investment in AI, infrastructure and private credit
items.description: Over the course of its financial year, Temasek recorded a gain of S$49 billion and a 10.5% total shareholder return.

items.title: China warns about AI risks with Anthropic's Claude Code
items.description: China said specific versions of Claude Code posed back-door vulnerabilities that could send sensitive information to a remote server.

items.title: Samsung-backed AI chip firm Rebellions targets IPO in South Korea next year, CEO tells CNBC
items.description: Sunghyun Park, CEO of Rebellions, told CNBC that the company is leaning toward a listing on the KOSPI over the KOSDAQ.

items.title: This former Apple executive is betting on Shenzhen, not Silicon Valley, to create the 'next Apple'
items.description: Startups building consumer electronics will have a better shot at becoming the next Apple in Shenzhen than in Silicon Valley, said Will Wang, CEO of China's smart-glasses startup.

items.title: Apple begins testing CXMT chips for devices sold in China, FT says
